decision.label: Secret Redaction Required
decision.reason: Generated Detroit Dynamo handoffs, public code, and owner review artifacts must not expose local API keys, Vercel project identifiers, provider secrets, deployment bypass tokens, or environment values.

rules.label: Local environment secret values stay local
rules.targetSurface: .env*, Vercel env, Appwrite env, local developer shell
requiredChecks: Read local env files only to build redaction fingerprints, never to publish values
Scan generated launch artifacts for exact local secret values
Record only counts, file paths, and rule ids in redaction reports

rules.label: Vercel project identifiers are redacted
rules.targetSurface: .vercel project/repo link files and launch handoffs
requiredChecks: Treat Vercel org ids and project ids as local deployment metadata
Scan launch docs and generated artifacts for exact Vercel project identifiers
Keep linked project counts and link type, but not ids, in handoff artifacts

rules.label: Appwrite server credentials are never in public handoffs
rules.targetSurface: Appwrite API keys, function variables, server SDK configuration
requiredChecks: Scan docs, scripts, source files, functions, and artifacts for exact Appwrite API key values
Allow client-safe VITE endpoint/project id references only where the frontend needs them
Keep server-key proof as yes/no evidence rather than printing values

rules.label: Payment provider secrets stay out of launch artifacts
rules.targetSurface: Stripe, PayPal, webhook, checkout, and refund configuration
requiredChecks: Scan for Stripe secret keys, webhook secrets, PayPal secrets, and payment tokens
Publish payment readiness as disconnected/approved status, not secret values
Keep checkout disabled until payment provider products and webhooks are approved

rules.label: Deployment access tokens and bypass secrets are not documented
rules.targetSurface: Preview deployment protection, Vercel token, CI token, deployment URLs
requiredChecks: Scan for committed Vercel tokens and deployment bypass secrets
Record preview access instructions without exposing bearer tokens or bypass values
Use Vercel CLI authentication or protected preview access instead of public tokens

rules.label: Generated launch artifacts are share-safe
requiredChecks: Scan JSON, Markdown, and CSV launch artifacts for exact secret values
Scan generated artifacts for high-confidence secret token patterns
Keep generated reports free of actual env values and deployment ids unless owner-approved

rules.label: Public source and documentation avoid secrets
rules.targetSurface: src, scripts, functions, docs, appwrite.json, package.json, vercel.json
requiredChecks: Scan source and docs for exact local secret values
Allow environment variable names without values
Reject raw tokens, provider secrets, or project ids in checked handoff text

rules.label: Owner handoffs use evidence labels instead of secrets
rules.targetSurface: Roadmap, owner signoff register, evidence intake, live readiness board
requiredChecks: Use proof-location fields instead of secret values
Record approver, date, and verification command without printing credentials
Keep launch approval blocked if redaction scan reports any leakage

usageRules: Use this report before sharing generated launch artifacts or owner-review handoffs.
Never write exact local secret values, Vercel project identifiers, deployment bypass tokens, or payment provider secrets into generated reports.
A nonzero leakage count blocks owner handoff sharing, production promotion, checkout activation, and live admin-write activation.
Environment variable names and redacted status labels are allowed; environment variable values are not.
